description_long: This object is a vintage folding bellows camera, featuring a textured black metal body with chrome-plated hardware and a collapsible accordion-style bellows. The front panel houses a circular lens assembly with engraved markings, a shutter speed selector, and a small viewfinder mounted to the side. The top of the camera includes a sturdy metal handle for portability, and the back panel is marked with a label and a latch for opening the film compartment. The camera's base is flat, designed to rest securely on surfaces, and the overall construction is robust, with visible rivets and mechanical linkages for extending and retracting the bellows. The design is utilitarian yet elegant, reflecting early to mid-20th-century photographic equipment.
description: A vintage folding bellows camera with a textured black metal body, chrome hardware, and a collapsible lens assembly. Features include a top handle, side viewfinder, and detailed lens controls.
description_view_0: Side view showing the extended bellows, chrome support struts, and the lens assembly protruding from the front.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the textured back panel, top handle, and a visible label with a latch for opening the film compartment.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the bellows, chrome hardware, and the round knob for extending or retracting the bellows.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the circular lens assembly, shutter speed selector, and the small side-mounted viewfinder.
